---
title: "Module 3 — MLE & Parameter Uncertainty"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

<!-- Thin module landing (repo convention: index.qmd is a landing, never a
     lecture deck). Decks live beside it; see CLAUDE.md. -->

The least-squares fit, re-derived from a linear-Gaussian noise model — and then
interrogated: is $\hat{\mathbf{w}}$ unique, is it unbiased, and how uncertain is
it? Curvature of the log-likelihood (the Fisher information) turns "uniqueness"
into a quantitative statement about parameter spread.

| Deck | Link |
|------|------|
| Lecture A — Likelihood & Maximum Likelihood | [m3a-likelihood-mle](m3a-likelihood-mle.html) |
| Lecture B — Properties of the MLE | [m3b-mle-uncertainty](m3b-mle-uncertainty.html) |
| Appendix — Full Cov[ŵ] derivation | [appendix-covariance](appendix-covariance.html) |

No peer loop this module — the [in-lecture parameter-uncertainty demo](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/m3-parameter-uncertainty/)
is embedded live in Lecture B (the week03 Bayesian-updating tool belongs to **Module 4**).

[← All slide decks](../../index.html)
